headlines.title: Bowen: 'Middle East is in turmoil' as Iran and Israel attack each other
headlines.description: The BBC's international editor, Jeremy Bowen, said the public are "right to be worried" as the consequences of the war will be felt for generations.

headlines.title: Starmer tells Apple and Google to ban nude images on children's phones
headlines.description: Firms will be expected to activate built-in features to stop children accessing sexually explicit images.

headlines.title: Nottingham attacks inquiry revealed miscarriage of justice, victim's mother says
headlines.description: The bereaved families of the victims of Valdo Calocane speak at a press conference in London.

headlines.title: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
headlines.description: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.

headlines.title: Christian Eriksen 'in good spirits' after collapsing in Denmark game, team's doctor says
headlines.description: Christian Eriksen is "in good spirits" following his collapse against Ukraine and is expected to be discharged from hospital soon, says Denmark's national team doctor Morten Boesen.

headlines.title: At least 32 dead after major earthquake strikes southern Philippines
headlines.description: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

headlines.title: Women lured by illegal sperm donor services on social media
headlines.description: A BBC investigation paid £100 for one donor’s “baby batter” delivered by post with a carton of passata.

headlines.title: Woman, 19, dies after gunshots heard at house
headlines.description: Detectives say they were called to the scene in north London just after midnight on Monday.

headlines.title: The top five-a-day foods your heart needs, according to new study
headlines.description: Not all fruit and veg is equal for getting nutrients called flavanols, say researchers.

headlines.title: You may be saving to give up work without realising it. Here's how to check
headlines.description: One simple check could ensure you are not missing out on free money which could help in later life.

headlines.title: My drinking days are over - here are my tips for an alcohol-free World Cup
headlines.description: Sober or sober-curious? One football fan shares his tips for having a great alcohol-free World Cup.

headlines.title: 'A World Cup for them not us': Fans' anger at US travel bans and visa restrictions
headlines.description: Fans across the world say US travel bans and visa regulations make them feel excluded from the World Cup.

headlines.title: How the High Street became a window on our political instability
headlines.description: High Streets have declined in recent years. What does this tell us about the UK?

headlines.title: What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
headlines.description: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

headlines.title: 'We don't look at the sky anymore': The Air India crash victims who were not on the plane
headlines.description: A grandfather, a survivor, a witness: one year after the crash, the people on the ground tell their stories.

headlines.title: I've been playing rugby since I was eight, I never thought about concussion - until now
headlines.description: A first-of-its-kind rugby study changes how players think about their health and future in the sport.

headlines.title: Friendship or leverage: Why is Xi Jinping in North Korea?
headlines.description: Beijing is trying to reassert influence over a strategically vital yet deeply unpredictable partner.

headlines.title: Starmer preparing to announce social media restrictions for children
headlines.description: The prime minister has pledged "decisive" measures in response to calls for an Australia-style ban.

headlines.title: DUP leader defends MP who attended Palestine solidarity counter-demonstration
headlines.description: Organisers said a counter-protest in Scarva, County Down, was "racist, xenophobic, sectarian".

headlines.title: John Lithgow and Lesley Manville lead Tony Award winners
headlines.description: A revival of Arthur Miller's Death of a Salesman was the night's big winner with six awards overall.

headlines.title: Spain's visitor numbers hit new highs as tourists avoid Middle East
headlines.description: The European country had 9.1 million international visitors in April, the most ever for that month.

headlines.title: Stormzy among stars remembering killed Grammy winner
headlines.description: The musician whose real name was Mark Orabiyi - was "a gem", rapper Wretch 32 said.

headlines.title: Stock markets fall on tech plunge and renewed Middle East attacks
headlines.description: Markets in Europe and Asia were hit by a tech sell-off, and oil rises as Iran and Israel launch attacks at each other

headlines.title: Six more due in court after Henry Nowak police protest
headlines.description: Disorder erupted in Southampton on Tuesday near where 18-year-old Henry Nowak was fatally stabbed.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: David Lammy Tells JD Vance He's Wrong Over Immigration Comments
headlines.description: The Deputy PM called the US Vice President and told him he’s wrong over Henry Nowak post.

headlines.title: Anthropic: AI could escape human control
headlines.description: US firm proposes coordinated global slowdown on building advanced artificial intelligence

headlines.title: Bellingham has fight to start for England - Tuchel
headlines.description: Jude Bellingham has a fight on his hands to feature in England's starting line-up at the World Cup, says manager Thomas Tuchel.

headlines.title: Crusaders suffer record 134-0 defeat
headlines.description: The scoreline was the biggest defeat of any side in the top two divisions in the 131-year history of rugby league.

headlines.title: Why Hull's US Open near-miss shows major win is close
headlines.description: BBC golf correspondent Iain Carter examines Charley Hull’s US Open performance after she came close to landing a first major title.

headlines.title: McCullum backs captain Stokes to find batting form
headlines.description: England captain Ben Stokes’ batting is “not too far away” from a return to form, says head coach Brendon McCullum.

headlines.title: How Williams' British hitting partner kept comeback secret
headlines.description: The British hitting partner of Serena Williams tells BBC Sport how he became involved in one of tennis' closest-guarded secrets.

headlines.title: Mourinho set for Real after Perez re-elected
headlines.description: Jose Mourinho is set to confirmed as Real Madrid's new head coach after Florentino Perez was re-elected as the club's president until 2030.
